Test_Case_Title: Claim description less than 20 characters rejected
Pre_Conditions: User is on new claim modal
Test_Steps: 1. Open File Claim modal | 2. Fill all fields | 3. Enter description < 20 chars | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Error: Description is required (min 20 characters).

Test_Case_Title: Claim description character counter updates correctly
Pre_Conditions: User is on new claim modal
Test_Steps: 1. Open File Claim modal | 2. Type in description field | 3. Observe counter
Expected_Result: Character counter shows X/500 and updates with each keystroke.

Test_Case_Title: Claim description max 500 characters enforced
Pre_Conditions: User is on new claim modal
Test_Steps: 1. Open File Claim modal | 2. Paste 501 character text in description
Expected_Result: Description field stops at 500 characters. Extra chars not accepted.

Test_Case_Title: Claim reference number generated after submission
Test_Steps: 1. Submit valid claim | 2. Observe toast message
Expected_Result: Toast shows: Claim submitted! Reference: CLM[ID]

Test_Case_Title: Submit claim without selecting policy
Pre_Conditions: User is on new claim modal
Test_Steps: 1. Open File Claim modal | 2. Leave Policy blank | 3. Fill other fields | 4. Click Submit
Expected_Result: Inline error on Policy field. Claim not submitted.

Test_Case_Title: Submit claim with empty incident date
Pre_Conditions: User is on new claim modal
Test_Steps: 1. Open File Claim modal | 2. Leave Date blank | 3. Fill other fields | 4. Click Submit
Expected_Result: Inline error on Date field. Claim not submitted.

Test_Case_Title: Track claim status displays status timeline
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to claims.html | 2. Click Track on a claim | 3. Observe detail modal
Expected_Result: Claim detail modal shows status timeline with dates for each status change.

Test_Case_Title: Approved claim shows Approved in timeline
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to claims.html | 2. Click Track on approved claim
Expected_Result: Timeline shows all steps with Approved as final completed state.

Test_Case_Title: Additional Info Required shows upload option
Pre_Conditions: Admin has set claim to Additional Info Required
Test_Steps: 1. Set a claim to Additional Info Required status | 2. Login as customer | 3. Track claim
Test_Data: Claim with Additional Info Required status
Expected_Result: Upload additional documents section visible in claim detail.

Test_Case_Title: All claims listed in claims history
Test_Steps: 1. Login | 2. Navigate to claims.html | 3. Verify all claims shown
Expected_Result: All user claims displayed in table with correct columns.

Test_Case_Title: File claim navigated from Policy Detail
Pre_Conditions: User is on policy-detail.html for active policy
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to policy-detail.html | 2. Click File Claim button | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Navigated to claims.html. Claim modal opens with policy pre-selected.

Test_Case_Title: No active policies - claim form shows empty dropdown
Test_Steps: 1. Login as user with only expired policies | 2. Open File Claim modal | 3. Check policy dropdown
Expected_Result: Policy dropdown empty. No active policies to select.

Test_Case_Title: All transactions displayed in payment history
Test_Steps: 1. Login | 2. Navigate to payments.html | 3. Verify transactions shown
Expected_Result: All transactions displayed: TxnID, Policy, Type, Date, Method, Amount, Status.

Test_Case_Title: Payment history search by transaction ID
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to payments.html | 2. Type transaction ID in search | 3. Observe filtered results
Expected_Result: Filtered results show only matching transaction.

Test_Case_Title: Payment history search by insurance type
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to payments.html | 2. Type Motor in search | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Only Motor insurance transactions shown.

Test_Case_Title: Download receipt opens receipt modal
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to payments.html | 2. Click receipt icon on a transaction | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Receipt modal opens with all transaction details.

Test_Case_Title: Receipt shows correct transaction details
Test_Steps: 1. Open receipt for specific transaction | 2. Verify all fields
Expected_Result: Receipt shows: TxnID, Reference No, Policy ID, Insurance Type, Date, Method, Status, Amount.

Test_Case_Title: Total premium paid stat shows correct amount
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to payments.html | 2. Check Total Premium Paid stat | 3. Sum payments manually
Expected_Result: Stat card shows ₹30,500 total premium paid.

Test_Case_Title: Payment history empty state for new user
Test_Steps: 1. Register new user | 2. Login | 3. Navigate to payments.html
Expected_Result: Empty state displayed: No transactions found.

Test_Case_Title: Policy documents listed for all policies
Test_Steps: 1. Login | 2. Navigate to documents.html | 3. Verify documents listed
Expected_Result: Documents listed: Policy Document, Premium Receipt, Renewal Notice per policy.

Test_Case_Title: Download policy document initiates download
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to documents.html | 2. Click Download on Policy Document | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: File download initiated. Document downloaded successfully.

Test_Case_Title: Preview button opens document preview
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to documents.html | 2. Click Preview on any document
Expected_Result: Preview action triggers. In demo, toast confirmation shown.

Test_Case_Title: Claim settlement letter available for approved claims
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to documents.html | 2. Check for Claim Settlement Letter | 3. Verify for approved claim
Expected_Result: Claim Settlement Letter appears in documents list for approved claims.

Test_Case_Title: Empty state shown when user has no policies
Test_Steps: 1. Register and login as new user | 2. Navigate to documents.html
Expected_Result: Empty state: No documents available. Link to buy policy shown.

Test_Case_Title: Admin login redirects to admin panel
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to login.html | 2. Login with admin credentials | 3. Observe redirect
Expected_Result: Redirected to admin.html. Admin badge visible in navbar.

Test_Case_Title: Customer login does NOT access admin panel
Test_Steps: 1. Login with customer credentials | 2. Try to navigate to admin.html directly
Expected_Result: Redirected to login.html or dashboard. Customer cannot access admin panel.

Test_Case_Title: Admin dashboard shows total customer count
Pre_Conditions: Admin is logged in on admin.html
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Check Customers stat card
Expected_Result: Customers stat card shows correct count of registered customers.

Test_Case_Title: Admin dashboard shows total policies count
Pre_Conditions: Admin is logged in on admin.html
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Check Total Policies stat card
Expected_Result: Total Policies stat card shows correct count across all users.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view all registered customers
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Click Customers section | 3. Verify customer list
Expected_Result: All non-admin customers displayed with Name, Email, Mobile, Joined date, Policy count.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can search customer by name
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Customers | 3. Type customer name in search
Expected_Result: Filtered results show matching customer Rahul Sharma.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can search customer by email
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Customers | 3. Search by email
Expected_Result: Filtered results show customer with matching email.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can search customer by mobile number
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Customers | 3. Search by mobile
Expected_Result: Filtered results show customer with matching mobile.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can deactivate a customer account
Pre_Conditions: Admin is on Customers section with active customer
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Customers | 3. Click Deactivate for a customer | 4. Verify status change
Expected_Result: Customer status changes to Locked. Button changes to Unlock.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can unlock a locked customer account
Pre_Conditions: Admin is on Customers section with locked customer
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Customers | 3. Click Unlock for locked customer
Expected_Result: Customer status changes back to Active.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view all claims across all customers
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Claims section | 3. Verify
Expected_Result: All claims from all customers displayed.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can update claim status to Approved
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Go to Claims | 3. Click Update on Under Review claim | 4. Select Approved | 5. Click Update Status
Expected_Result: Claim status updated to Approved. Table refreshes showing new status.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can update claim status to Rejected
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Go to Claims | 3. Click Update | 4. Select Rejected | 5. Submit
Expected_Result: Claim status changed to Rejected.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can set claim to Additional Info Required
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Click Update on a claim | 3. Select Additional Info Required | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: Claim status changed. Customer can now see upload option in tracking.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view Policies Sold by Type report
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Reports section | 3. Check Policies Sold by Type
Expected_Result: Progress bar chart showing policy count per type (Motor/Health/Home/Travel) visible.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view Claims by Status report
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Reports | 3. Check Claims by Status
Expected_Result: Bar chart showing claims count for each status (Submitted/Approved/Rejected etc).

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view Premium Collection Summary
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to Reports | 3. Check Premium Collection Summary
Expected_Result: Summary shows: Total collected, transaction count, avg premium per policy.

Test_Case_Title: Admin can view all policies across all customers
Test_Steps: 1. Login as admin | 2. Navigate to All Policies section | 3. Verify
Expected_Result: All policies across all customers displayed with customer name, type, premium, status.

Test_Case_Title: Dashboard page load time within 3 seconds
Pre_Conditions: User is logged in on standard broadband
Test_Steps: 1. Login | 2. Measure time from navigation click to full page load of dashboard
Expected_Result: Dashboard fully loads in under 3 seconds.

Test_Case_Title: Application displays correctly on mobile screen (320px)
Test_Steps: 1. Open application on mobile (320px width) or resize browser to 320px | 2. Navigate through key pages | 3. Check layout
Expected_Result: Application is fully usable. No horizontal overflow. Text readable. Buttons tappable.

Test_Case_Title: Application displays correctly on tablet (768px)
Test_Steps: 1. Open application at 768px width | 2. Navigate through key pages
Expected_Result: Application layout adjusts correctly for tablet view.

Test_Case_Title: Application works correctly on Chrome
Test_Steps: 1. Open application in Chrome | 2. Complete registration, login, get quote flow | 3. Verify no errors
Expected_Result: All features work correctly without JS errors.

Test_Case_Title: Application works correctly on Firefox
Test_Steps: 1. Open application in Firefox | 2. Complete key user flows
Expected_Result: All features work correctly.

Test_Case_Title: Application works correctly on Edge
Test_Steps: 1. Open application in Microsoft Edge | 2. Complete key user flows
Expected_Result: All features work correctly.

Test_Case_Title: Password fields masked by default
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to register.html or login.html | 2. Click in password field | 3. Type password
Expected_Result: Password characters displayed as dots/asterisks by default.

Test_Case_Title: Card details not persisted in localStorage
Pre_Conditions: User completes checkout with card details
Test_Steps: 1. Complete checkout with credit card | 2. Open browser DevTools | 3. Check localStorage for card numbers
Expected_Result: No card numbers stored in localStorage. REQ-060 compliance.

Test_Case_Title: Error messages displayed in red color
Pre_Conditions: Application is on any form page
Test_Steps: 1. Submit any form with blank required fields | 2. Observe error message color
Expected_Result: All error messages displayed in red (#c62828 or similar). Consistent across pages.

Test_Case_Title: Success messages displayed in green color
Pre_Conditions: Application is on any form page
Test_Steps: 1. Complete any successful action (register, save quote, etc) | 2. Observe toast color
Expected_Result: All success messages/toasts displayed in green. Consistent across pages.

Test_Case_Title: Date format DD/MM/YYYY used consistently
Pre_Conditions: Application is on any page with dates
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to dashboard, policies, claims | 2. Observe all date displays
Expected_Result: Dates consistently displayed in DD/MM/YYYY format (e.g., 01 Jan 2026).

Test_Case_Title: All form fields have visible labels and placeholders
Pre_Conditions: Application is on any form page
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to register, login, quote forms | 2. Inspect all form fields
Expected_Result: Every input field has a visible label above and a placeholder text inside.

Test_Case_Title: Direct URL access to dashboard without login redirects
Test_Steps: 1. Clear all cookies/localStorage | 2. Try to directly navigate to dashboard.html
Expected_Result: User redirected to login.html.

Test_Case_Title: Direct URL access to admin.html by customer redirects
Test_Steps: 1. Login as customer | 2. Navigate directly to admin.html URL
Expected_Result: Customer redirected to login.html or dashboard.html. Admin panel inaccessible.

Test_Case_Title: Complete E2E flow: Register > Login > Get Quote > Buy Policy
Test_Steps: 1. Register new user | 2. Login | 3. Get motor insurance quote | 4. Select plan | 5. Buy Now | 6. Complete checkout | 7. Verify policy in My Policies | 8. Verify payment in Payment History
Expected_Result: Policy created and visible in My Policies. Payment record in Payment History.

Test_Case_Title: Complete E2E flow: Login > File Claim > Admin Updates > Customer Tracks
Pre_Conditions: User has active policy | Admin account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Login as customer | 2. File claim on active policy | 3. Login as admin | 4. Update claim to Approved | 5. Login as customer | 6. Track claim | 7. Verify Approved status
Expected_Result: Claim shows Approved in status timeline for customer.

Test_Case_Title: Complete E2E flow: Buy Policy > File Claim > Download Documents
Test_Steps: 1. Buy a health policy | 2. File a claim | 3. Admin approves claim | 4. Check documents for settlement letter
Expected_Result: Claim settlement letter appears in Documents after approval.

Test_Case_Title: Policy bought appears in Payments with correct amount
Test_Steps: 1. Buy any policy | 2. Navigate to payments.html | 3. Verify payment record
Expected_Result: Payment record shows correct amount ₹12500 for purchased policy.

Test_Case_Title: Policy cancellation reflected immediately in policies list
Test_Steps: 1. Cancel a policy | 2. Navigate to policies.html | 3. Check status
Expected_Result: Policy shows Cancelled status immediately in list view.
